質問: What is required to become a private pilot?
模範回答: Must be at least 17 years old / Read, write, and speak English / Pass a written knowledge exam / Have adequate Ground and Flight training endorsements / Hold at least a third class medical

質問: What are your privileges and limitations?
模範回答: Privileges: Can demonstrate aircraft to buyer (salesperson/broker with 200hrs) / Can tow glider as PIC / Can act as PIC incidental to business (no passengers for compensation). Limitations: Cannot pay less than pro-rated share of expenses / Cannot fly for hire or compensation

質問: How do you remain current as a Private Pilot?
模範回答: Complete a Flight Review every 2 years. To carry passengers: within preceding 90 days, complete 3 takeoffs and landings in aircraft of same category, class, and type. Night: must be full stop, between 1hr after sunset and 1hr before sunrise. FAR 61.56 and 61.57

質問: What documents are required aboard an aircraft?
模範回答: Airworthiness Certification / Registration / Radio Certification (International Only) / Owner's Manual / Weight and Balance. FAR 91.203 and 91.9

質問: Does the registration ever expire?
模範回答: Yes, every 3 years the owner must reregister

質問: What documents must the PIC always have with them?
模範回答: Medical Certificate / Student/Private Pilot Certificate / Photo ID

質問: What inspections are required for your aircraft?
模範回答: AVIATE: A-Airworthiness Directives / V-VOR Check (every 30 days, IFR only) / I-Inspections: 100hr (for hire) and Annual (all aircraft) / A-Altimeter/Pitot Static (every 24 calendar months, IFR only) / T-Transponder (every 24 calendar months) / E-ELT (every 12 calendar months). FAR 91.409

質問: Difference between 100 hour inspection and Annual inspection?
模範回答: All aircraft must have Annual. Aircraft for hire must also have 100hr. Annual is more in-depth and requires more certified mechanic. Annual CAN replace 100hr, but 100hr CANNOT replace Annual.

質問: What is an MEL?
模範回答: Minimum Equipment List. Equipment that is allowed to be broken but the flight can still proceed.

質問: What is a special flight permit?
模範回答: Gives temporary airworthiness to aircraft without current airworthiness. Usually one day to a certain airport. Issued by FSDO (Flight Standards District Office), normally when going to see a mechanic.

質問: Explain the three types of medicals
模範回答: Third Class: Least extensive, every pilot must have at least this. Under 40: 60 months / Over 40: 24 calendar months. Second Class: Required for Commercial Pilots. First Class: Most extensive, required for ATPs.

質問: What is hypoxia?
模範回答: Not getting enough oxygen to the brain

質問: What are the 4 types of hypoxia?
模範回答: 1. Hypoxic Hypoxia: not enough oxygen, high altitudes over 12,500ft (most common) / 2. Hypemic Hypoxia: enough oxygen but not enough good blood to carry it (anemia or CO poisoning) / 3. Stagnant Hypoxia: heart pump not working / 4. Histotoxic Hypoxia: oxygen available but cells cannot absorb it (being drunk)

質問: Symptoms of hypoxia
模範回答: Headache, dizziness, skin turning blue, euphoria

質問: Explain hyperventilation
模範回答: Overbreathing / breathing too rapidly. Breathing out CO2 too quickly. Treatment: breathe into a bag or calm yourself. Symptoms: headache, dizziness, skin turning blue, euphoria

質問: Name and explain the 8 types of spatial disorientation
模範回答: State of confusion, losing orientation to the horizon. 1. The Leans: illusion of turning opposite direction / 2. Coriolis: making quick head movement / 3. Graveyard Spin: recovering from spin with illusion of entering spin other direction / 4. Graveyard Spiral: turning illusion / 5. Inversion Illusion: climb to level flight / 6. Elevator Illusion: updraft illusion / 7. False Horizon: from city lights or clouds / 8. AutoKinesis: lights appear to move at night

質問: What is standard temperature and pressure?
模範回答: 15 degrees Celsius / 29.92 inches of mercury

質問: What are the 3 main layers of the atmosphere?
模範回答: Troposphere / Tropopause / Stratosphere

質問: The cause of all weather is a result of a:
模範回答: Heat exchange - causes atmospheric circulation

質問: Wind is the result of:
模範回答: Changes or differences in pressure

質問: Name and explain the 4 main types of fog
模範回答: 1. Radiation Fog: clear calm night, no wind, land area, stays stationary / 2. Advection Fog: warm moist air over cool surface, requires light breeze, sea fogs always advection / 3. Upslope Fog: light wind pushes moist air up hillside, covers large area / 4. Steam Fog: cold air moves over warm water, looks like wisps of smoke

質問: Difference between stable and unstable air
模範回答: Stable: calm, stratiform clouds, continuous precipitation, smooth air, poor to fair visibility. Unstable: turbulent, cumulus clouds, showery precipitation, good visibility, strong surface winds

質問: What is the difference between an air mass and a front?
模範回答: Air mass: large body of air with similar temperature and moisture properties. Front: transition zone between two air masses of different density, can be horizontal and vertical.

質問: Name the 4 main types of fronts
模範回答: Warm Front: leading edge of warm air replacing cold air / Cold Front: leading edge of cold air replacing warm air / Stationary Front: front that is not moving / Occluded Front: cold front catches up to a warm front

質問: What causes turbulence?
模範回答: Convective currents / Obstructions to wind flow / Wind shear

質問: Name and explain the 3 types of icing
模範回答: Clear: hard and glossy after drops freeze over airfoil / Rime: brittle and frostlike, small drops freezing rapidly / Mixed: hard and rough, drops vary in size or snow mixed with rain

質問: What 3 ingredients must be present for a thunderstorm to develop?
模範回答: Sufficient water vapor / Unstable lapse rate / Uplifting action to start the process in motion

質問: What are the 3 stages of a thunderstorm?
模範回答: Cumulus: initial updrafts / Mature: beginning of falling precipitation (most dangerous) / Dissipating: downdrafts as thunderstorm begins dissipating

質問: What are isobars?
模範回答: Lines on a weather chart of equal pressure

質問: How long are TAFs valid for?
模範回答: 24 hour period

質問: When are TAFs issued?
模範回答: 4 times daily

質問: When are PIREPs issued?
模範回答: Issued by the pilot for turbulence, visibility, icing

質問: When are METARs issued?
模範回答: 55 minutes past the hour, usually only good for an hour

質問: What are prognostic charts?
模範回答: Issued 4 times daily, provides 12hr and 24hr forecast up to 24,000ft. Displays likely weather forecast for future time including temperature, wind, precipitation and fronts.

質問: What is a surface analysis chart?
模範回答: Depicts weather conditions from a few hours earlier. Developed every 3 hours. Shows atmospheric pressure, high/low pressure systems, frontal movements.

質問: What is a weather depiction chart?
模範回答: Computer generated chart based on METAR reports giving broad overview of flying conditions. Updated every 3 hours, valid only at time issued.

質問: What is the difference between an AIRMET, a SIGMET, and a CONVECTIVE SIGMET?
模範回答: SIGMET: severe/extreme conditions for ALL AIRCRAFT (icing, turbulence, dust storms, volcanic ash) / Convective SIGMET: issued for sufficiently strong, widespread, or embedded thunderstorms / AIRMET: less severe conditions hazardous to smaller aircraft

質問: What are the 3 types of AIRMETs?
模範回答: Sierra: IFR conditions or mountain obscurations / Tango: turbulence and surface winds greater than 30 knots / Zulu: icing conditions

質問: What is a center weather advisory?
模範回答: Issued by control centers, concerns both SIGMET and AIRMET conditions, relates to specific air route traffic control center areas

質問: Define the term Ceiling
模範回答: The lowest broken or overcast layer of clouds

質問: Which is worst, SIGMET or AIRMET?
模範回答: SIGMET - extreme conditions that relate to all aircraft

質問: What 4 forces are always acting on an airplane?
模範回答: Lift / Weight / Drag / Thrust

質問: What is angle of attack?
模範回答: The angle between the chord line and relative wind

質問: What is relative wind?
模範回答: The direction of airflow with respect to an airfoil. Parallel and opposite the path of the airfoil's movement.

質問: What is Bernoulli's principle?
模範回答: When a fluid's velocity increases, the pressure decreases. Produces lift as more pressure with slower moving air under the airfoil.

質問: What is a stall?
模範回答: Exceed critical angle of attack. Abrupt decrease in lift because there is no more airflow over the wings.

質問: What factors affect stall speed?
模範回答: Weight and center of gravity

質問: Characteristics of forward and aft CG
模範回答: Aft CG: less stable, higher cruise speed, lower stall speed. Forward CG: more stable, slower cruise speed, higher stall speed.

質問: What is P-factor?
模範回答: Prop turns clockwise viewed from pilot's seat. Descending blade moves faster than ascending blade, making aircraft yaw left.

質問: What are the 2 types of drag?
模範回答: Parasite drag: all things that slow the aircraft (form, skin, interference drag) / Induced drag: byproduct of lift

質問: What is load factor?
模範回答: The load the wings are supporting divided by the total weight of the airplane. Ratio of lift generated by wings / total weight. Usually expressed in G's.

質問: What effect does an increase in load factor have on stall speed?
模範回答: A heavily loaded airplane has a higher stall speed. Must use higher AOA to generate required lift, reaching critical AOA at faster airspeed.

質問: Define VA
模範回答: VA is maneuvering speed. The speed at which maneuvers can be performed without over stressing the airplane.

質問: What is the relationship between maneuvering speed and weight?
模範回答: Maneuvering speed decreases at lighter weight. Lighter airplane = lower Va because turbulence can more easily displace it.

質問: What causes a spin?
模範回答: Both wings are stalled, but one is more stalled than the other

質問: What is proper spin recovery?
模範回答: Power idle / Ailerons neutral / Full opposite rudder / Forward pressure on elevator

質問: When are spins most likely to occur?
模範回答: Airplane must be stalled - anytime in flight that you meet or exceed the critical angle of attack

質問: Define Ground Effect
模範回答: The reduction of induced drag due to interference of the ground over the wings. Looks and feels like an increase in lift.

質問: What is adverse yaw?
模範回答: The tendency of an airplane to yaw opposite the direction it is rolling

質問: What is density altitude?
模範回答: The pressure altitude corrected for nonstandard temperature variations. Air density is the single most important factor to aircraft performance.

質問: What factors affect density altitude?
模範回答: Increased humidity increases DA (decreases performance) / Increased temperature increases DA / Increased pressure/elevation increases DA

質問: VA - Maneuvering speed
模範回答: 97 knots at 2300 lbs

質問: VFE - Maximum flap extended speed
模範回答: 85 knots

質問: VNE - Never exceed speed
模範回答: 160 knots

質問: VX - Best angle of climb
模範回答: 59 knots

質問: VY - Best rate of climb
模範回答: 73 knots

質問: White arc on airspeed indicator
模範回答: Flap operating range: 41-85 knots

質問: Green arc on airspeed indicator
模範回答: Normal operating range: 47-128 knots

質問: Yellow arc on airspeed indicator
模範回答: Caution range: fly only in smooth air: 128-160 knots

質問: Red line on airspeed indicator
模範回答: Never exceed speed: 160 knots

質問: What is indicated airspeed?
模範回答: Type of airspeed read directly from the airspeed indicator driven by a pitot-static system

質問: What is calibrated airspeed?
模範回答: Indicated airspeed corrected for instrument error

質問: What is true airspeed?
模範回答: The actual speed of the aircraft through the air. Must calculate based on indicated airspeed, current pressure altitude, and air temperature.

質問: Do we use our cones or rods to see at night?
模範回答: Rods at night, Cones during the day

質問: What can you do to protect your eyesight at night?
模範回答: Avoid bright lights / Use red aviation flashlight / Dim the lights in the cockpit

質問: When are position lights required for night flight?
模範回答: At all times during night flight

質問: When are anti-collision lights required for night flight?
模範回答: At all times during night flight

質問: What color are taxiway lights?
模範回答: Blue

質問: What are REILs?
模範回答: Runway End Identifier Lights

質問: What color are airport beacons?
模範回答: White and green: lighted land airport / White and yellow: lighted sea airport / Green, yellow, white: lighted heliport / White, white, green: lighted military airport

質問: How do you activate airport lights at night?
模範回答: Controlled by radio on UNICOM or designated frequency. Pilot Controlled: 3 clicks=low, 5 clicks=medium, 7 clicks=high intensity

質問: What are the 4 control surfaces?
模範回答: Ailerons / Elevator / Rudder / Flaps

質問: What is the function of the flaps?
模範回答: Increase lift and induced drag at any AOA. Greater lift for short field takeoffs, greater drag for landing. Permits slower airspeed and steeper angle of descent during approach.

質問: What are the 4 strokes of an engine?
模範回答: Intake / Compression / Power / Exhaust (4 strokes, 5 events - Ignition added)

質問: How does the carb heat system work?
模範回答: Unfiltered air off the exhaust manifold that helps melt any carb ice issues

質問: Why do we experience a drop in RPM when applying carb heat?
模範回答: Hot air is less dense, enriching fuel-air mixture, causing decrease in engine performance

質問: What does the throttle control?
模範回答: The amount of air allowed into the induction system

質問: What does the mixture control?
模範回答: Sets the amount of fuel available to the engine for combustion. Used with throttle to control fuel-to-air mixture for best performance at altitude.

質問: Which instruments operate on a gyroscope?
模範回答: Heading indicator and Attitude indicator (vacuum gyros) / Turn coordinator (electrical gyro)

質問: What are the two main fundamentals of a gyroscope?
模範回答: Rigidity in Space: Newton's first law, gyroscope remains in fixed position while spinning. Precession: change in orientation of a rotating body.

質問: Name the compass errors
模範回答: Turning errors: Undershoot North, Overshoot South (NoSF). Dip errors: Accelerate North, Decelerate South (ANDS)

質問: How does the pitot-static system work?
模範回答: Operates on pressure differential. Airspeed: compares static to ram pressure / VSI: compares changes in pressure to calibrated leak / Altimeter: compares static pressure to reference pressure set in Kollsman window

質問: What is the first indication of carb ice?
模範回答: A drop in RPM

質問: Define detonation and preignition
模範回答: Detonation: uncontrolled firing of fuel-air mixture, caused by excessive temperature or lower grade fuel. Preignition: fuel-air mixture ignites prematurely, usually from hot spot/carbon deposit on spark plug.

質問: What procedures following a partial loss of power?
模範回答: Treat as complete loss of power. ABC's: Airspeed 65 knots / Best landing area, stay committed / Checklist

質問: What procedure following engine fire in flight?
模範回答: Consult POH / Mixture idle cut off / Fuel shut off valve off / Master switch off / Cabin heat off / Airspeed into emergency descent

質問: What does low oil pressure mean?
模範回答: Very bad sign, something is wrong. Land as soon as practical.

質問: In the event of alternator failure, how long will battery last?
模範回答: No more than 30 minutes

質問: If vacuum system failed, which instruments will you lose?
模範回答: Heading indicator and Attitude indicator

質問: What is CFIT?
模範回答: Controlled Flight Into Terrain. Flying a perfectly good airplane into terrain because of lack of situational awareness or visibility.

質問: What is Class A Airspace?
模範回答: Not shown on sectional, covers entire nation, starts at 18,000 MSL. IFR ONLY.

質問: What is Class B airspace?
模範回答: Surrounds busiest airports, usually up to 10,000ft MSL. Solid blue line on sectional. Requires: 2-way radio communication, ATC clearance, Mode C transponder, PPL or endorsement.

質問: What are the VFR cloud clearances for Class B airspace?
模範回答: 3SM visibility and clear of clouds

質問: What is Class C airspace?
模範回答: Surrounds medium airports. Solid magenta line with at least 2 different layers. Requires: 2-way radio communication, Mode C transponder.

質問: What are the VFR visibility and cloud clearance requirements for Class C airspace?
模範回答: 3SM visibility / 1000ft above / 500ft below / 2000ft horizontally

質問: What is Class D airspace?
模範回答: Relatively small, surrounds tower airports. Dashed blue line. Reverts to Class E when tower is closed. Requires: 2-way radio communication.

質問: What are the VFR visibility and cloud clearance requirements for Class D airspace?
模範回答: 3SM visibility / 1000ft above / 500ft below / 2000ft horizontally

質問: What is Class E airspace?
模範回答: Can start anywhere from surface up to 17,999ft MSL. Controlled airspace.

質問: What are the VFR visibility and cloud clearance requirements for Class E airspace?
模範回答: Below 10,000: 3SM visibility, 1000ft above, 500ft below, 2000ft horizontally. Above 10,000 MSL: 5SM visibility, 1000ft above, 1000ft below, 1 mile horizontally.

質問: What is Class G airspace?
模範回答: Completely uncontrolled, low-lying airspace

質問: What are the VFR visibility and cloud clearance requirements for Class G airspace?
模範回答: Daytime: 1SM visibility, clear of clouds. Nighttime: 3SM visibility, 1000ft above, 500ft below, 2000ft horizontally.

質問: Name the different types of special use airspace
模範回答: MCPRAWN: Military Operations Area (magenta hashmarks) / Controlled Firing Area / Prohibited Area (blue hash) / Restricted Area (blue hash) / Alert Area (magenta) / Warning Area (blue hash) / National Security TFR

質問: When do you need a Mode C transponder?
模範回答: Class A, B, within 30NM of Class B, in and above Class C, at or above 10,000MSL or 2,500AGL. If you have a transponder, it must be on.

質問: Define an aircraft accident
模範回答: Occurrence associated with operation of aircraft between boarding with intention of flight and everyone getting off. Involves death, serious injury, or substantial aircraft damage.

質問: Define an aircraft incident
模範回答: An occurrence other than an accident associated with aircraft operation where effects could have an effect on safety of operations.

質問: When is immediate notification of the NTSB required?
模範回答: Aircraft accident or incidents involving: flight control system malfunction / crew member unable to perform duties due to injury or illness / inflight fire / aircraft collision / property damage over $25,000

質問: When should you file a report if asked by the NTSB?
模範回答: Within 10 days

質問: What restrictions concern pilots in the use of alcohol?
模範回答: BAC .04 / Cannot drink within 8 hours before flight

質問: What preflight action is to be taken before each flight?
模範回答: PIC must be familiar with: weather reports and forecasts / fuel requirements / alternate airports / known traffic delays / runway lengths / takeoff and landing distances

質問: What are the VFR fuel requirements for day and night?
模範回答: Day: fuel to first point plus 30 minutes. Night: fuel to first point plus 45 minutes.

質問: What VFR instruments are required for day? night?
模範回答: Day (ATOMATOFLAMES): Airspeed, Tachometer, Oil pressure, Manifold pressure, Altimeter, Temperature gauge, Oil temp, Fuel gauge, Landing gear indicator, Anti-collision lights, Magnetic compass, ELT, Safety belts. Night adds: Fuses, Landing light, Anticollision lights, Position lights, Source of electricity.

質問: What is an ELT?
模範回答: Emergency Locator Transmitter. Used to locate aircraft in emergency.

質問: What are the requirements regarding the use of supplemental oxygen?
模範回答: 12,500-14,000ft: crew must use if over 30 minutes / 14,000-15,000ft: crew must use at all times / 15,000ft and above: passengers must be given access.

質問: What is a VASI?
模範回答: Visual Approach Slope Indicator. Lighting system to maintain safe glide path.

質問: What is a PAPI?
模範回答: Precision Approach Path Indicator. Lighting system to maintain safe glide path. Works like the VASI.

質問: What is wake turbulence?
模範回答: Turbulent wake produced by heavy aircraft primarily from wingtip vortices at high AOA (low speed). Byproduct of lift.

質問: How to avoid wake turbulence?
模範回答: Takeoff: takeoff before wake turbulence path and stay above. Landing: stay above and land further than other aircraft.

質問: What is LAHSO?
模範回答: Land and Hold Short Operations

質問: Before departure, what should passengers be briefed on?
模範回答: No smoking / Seatbelts and how to use / Location of exits / Location of survival equipment / Emergency procedures

質問: Explain the light gun signals
模範回答: Steady green: clear to land / Flashing green: clear to taxi / Steady red: continue circling/stop / Flashing red: airport unsafe, do not land / Flashing white: return to starting point on ground / Alternating red and green: use extreme caution

質問: What are the 2 most common ways to navigate?
模範回答: Pilotage: visual references to landmarks / Dead reckoning: deriving position mathematically from last known position

質問: What types of charts would you use for basic VFR navigation?
模範回答: Sectional chart and terminal area chart

質問: What is an isogonic line?
模範回答: Lines of equal magnetic variation

質問: What is magnetic variation?
模範回答: The difference between magnetic north and true north

質問: How do you convert true to magnetic?
模範回答: Minus east variation / Add west variation (East is Least, West is Best)

質問: What is a VOR?
模範回答: Very High Frequency Omni Range. Can fly directly to VOR and intercept different courses to or from station. 360 radials.

質問: What is an NDB?
模範回答: Non-Directional Beacon. Radio transmitter at known location. Going out of style.

質問: What is RNAV?
模範回答: Area Navigation. Allows aircraft to fly beyond a network of navigational aids.

質問: What is GPS?
模範回答: Global Positioning System. Uses satellites to establish position. Need 3 to triangulate, 4th to verify.

質問: What are the different methods for checking the VOR for accuracy?
模範回答: On the ground: plus or minus 4 degrees / In the air: plus or minus 6 degrees / Cross-referencing 2 VORs on separate radios

質問: What is DME?
模範回答: Distance Measuring Equipment. Tells you how far you are from the VOR.

質問: What are the 4 Cs of the lost procedures?
模範回答: Confess / Communicate / Climb / Conserve / Comply

質問: What is CRM?
模範回答: Crew Resource Management - how well you work with your crew

質問: What are the different types of altitude?
模範回答: Indicated: read directly from altimeter / True: actual altitude above sea level / Absolute: actual altitude above terrain / Pressure: altimeter set to 29.92 / Density: pressure altitude corrected for nonstandard temperature
